AU.name: Australia
AU.fact: Australia's bush food ingredients like wattleseed and lemon myrtle have been used by Aboriginal peoples for over 60,000 years

BE.name: Belgium
BE.fact: Belgium's Trappist ales are brewed within monastery walls under strict monastic oversight

BO.name: Bolivia
BO.fact: Bolivia grows over 200 varieties of native potatoes and was a center of peanut domestication

BR.name: Brazil
BR.fact: The Amazon basin in Brazil is the origin of cassava, cashew, and guarana

CA.name: Canada
CA.fact: Quebec produces over 70% of the world's maple syrup

CH.name: Switzerland
CH.fact: Swiss Gruyère cheese requires 400 liters of milk to make a single 35kg wheel

CL.name: Chile
CL.fact: Merkén is a smoked chili condiment from the Mapuche people of southern Chile

CN.name: China
CN.fact: China is the origin of rice, soybean, tea, and Sichuan peppercorn — all domesticated thousands of years ago

DE.name: Germany
DE.fact: German sauerkraut tradition dates to the 16th century; Captain Cook used it to prevent scurvy at sea

ES.name: Spain
ES.fact: Spanish Pimentón de la Vera has DOP status — the peppers are smoked over oak for two weeks

FI.name: Finland
FI.fact: Karelian pasties (karjalanpiirakka) are a Finnish national dish dating back centuries

FR.name: France
FR.fact: France's five great cheeses — Camembert, Roquefort, Brie, Comté, and Reblochon — all have protected origins

GB.name: United Kingdom
GB.fact: Stilton cheese can only legally be made in three English counties: Derbyshire, Leicestershire, and Nottinghamshire

GH.name: Ghana
GH.fact: Ghana is within the domestication zone of both yam and oil palm, two pillars of West African cuisine

GR.name: Greece
GR.fact: Greek feta cheese has been made for thousands of years and holds EU Protected Designation of Origin

ID.name: Indonesia
ID.fact: Indonesia's Maluku 'Spice Islands' are the native home of clove and nutmeg, once worth more than gold

IE.name: Ireland
IE.fact: Irish soda bread was born from necessity — baking soda was cheaper than yeast in 1830s Ireland

IN.name: India
IN.fact: India is the origin of black pepper, cardamom, turmeric, ginger, sesame, and eggplant

IR.name: Iran
IR.fact: Iran produces over 90% of the world's saffron and is the origin of pistachio and pomegranate

JP.name: Japan
JP.fact: Wasabi is native to Japan's mountain streams and loses its flavor within 15 minutes of grating

KR.name: South Korea
KR.fact: Korean gochujang is fermented for months in earthenware pots called onggi

KW.name: Kuwait
KW.fact: Kuwaiti cuisine features Baharat spice blends and Kabsa rice dishes

MX.name: Mexico
MX.fact: Mexico domesticated corn, chili, vanilla, cacao, tomato, avocado, squash, and black bean — reshaping global cuisine

NG.name: Nigeria
NG.fact: Nigeria is the world's largest producer of yams, with over 45 million tonnes annually

NL.name: Netherlands
NL.fact: Gouda accounts for over 50% of all cheese produced in the Netherlands

NO.name: Norway
NO.fact: Norwegian brunost (brown cheese) is made by slowly caramelizing whey — a process unique to Scandinavia

NZ.name: New Zealand
NZ.fact: New Zealand cuisine blends Māori traditions with global influences

PE.name: Peru
PE.fact: Peru domesticated the potato around 8000 BC — the Inca grew over 3,000 varieties

PH.name: Philippines
PH.fact: Calamansi, a citrus native to the Philippines, is essential to Filipino cooking and the national fruit

SE.name: Sweden
SE.fact: Surströmming, Sweden's fermented herring, is so pungent that some airlines have banned it

SG.name: Singapore
SG.fact: Singapore's laksa, Hainanese chicken rice, and chili crab reflect its multicultural food heritage

TH.name: Thailand
TH.fact: Thai Bird's Eye Chili is one of the hottest common culinary peppers, central to Thai cooking

TR.name: Turkey
TR.fact: Anatolia (modern Turkey) is where rye, chickpea, and cherry were first domesticated

US.name: United States
US.fact: The potato chip was invented in Saratoga Springs, New York in 1853

AF.name: Afghanistan
AF.fact: Kabuli Pulao, a fragrant rice dish with lamb and raisins, is Afghanistan's national dish

AR.name: Argentina
AR.fact: Argentina's chimichurri sauce originated with gauchos who needed a portable condiment for grilled meats

AM.name: Armenia
AM.fact: Armenian lavash flatbread was inscribed on UNESCO's intangible heritage list in 2014

BD.name: Bangladesh
BD.fact: Panch Phoron is a unique Bengali five-seed spice blend — the only one that uses whole spices unmixed

CO.name: Colombia
CO.fact: Colombian arepa flatbreads predate European contact and remain a daily staple

CR.name: Costa Rica
CR.fact: Salsa Lizano is so iconic in Costa Rica that it appears on nearly every restaurant table

CU.name: Cuba
CU.fact: Cuban mojo sauce, made with sour orange and garlic, defines the island's roast pork tradition

GE.name: Georgia
GE.fact: Khmeli Suneli is Georgia's signature spice blend, used in nearly every traditional dish

HT.name: Haiti
HT.fact: Pikliz, Haiti's spicy pickled cabbage relish, accompanies nearly every meal on the island

HU.name: Hungary
HU.fact: Hungarian smoked paprika (füstölt paprika) gets its distinctive flavor from slow oak-smoking

IQ.name: Iraq
IQ.fact: Ancient Mesopotamia (modern Iraq) was where the date palm was first cultivated over 6,000 years ago

IT.name: Italy
IT.fact: Parmigiano-Reggiano has been made in Emilia-Romagna for over 900 years with the same methods

LR.name: Liberia
LR.fact: Liberian cassava leaf stew is a national dish, though cassava itself originated in South America

MN.name: Mongolia
MN.fact: Buuz, Mongolian steamed dumplings, are the centerpiece of Tsagaan Sar (Lunar New Year) celebrations

MM.name: Myanmar
MM.fact: Mohinga, a fish-based rice noodle soup, is Myanmar's de facto national dish eaten at breakfast

NE.name: Niger
NE.fact: Niger is in the Sahel, where pearl millet was first domesticated as a drought-resistant grain

PK.name: Pakistan
PK.fact: Pakistani garam masala blends differ from Indian versions, often heavier on cardamom and cinnamon

PL.name: Poland
PL.fact: Polish kiełbasa sausage-making traditions date back to the Middle Ages

PR.name: Puerto Rico
PR.fact: Puerto Rican sofrito is the aromatic base of the island's cuisine, distinct from Cuban and Spanish versions

RO.name: Romania
RO.fact: Telemea is Romania's signature brined cheese, traditionally made from sheep's milk

RU.name: Russia
RU.fact: Russian smoked sturgeon and caviar have been prized delicacies since the tsarist era

ZA.name: South Africa
ZA.fact: Rooibos tea is endemic to South Africa's Cederberg region and grows nowhere else on Earth

UG.name: Uganda
UG.fact: Matoke, steamed green banana mash, is Uganda's staple food and cultural touchstone

UA.name: Ukraine
UA.fact: Borscht, Ukraine's iconic beet soup, was added to UNESCO's intangible heritage list in 2022

TW.name: Taiwan
TW.fact: Taiwanese soy paste (doubanjiang) is a fermented staple distinct from Chinese and Japanese versions

CZ.name: Czech Republic and Slovakia
CZ.fact: Czech pilsner, invented in Plzeň in 1842, revolutionized beer worldwide

DZ.name: Algeria
DZ.fact: Couscous was inscribed on UNESCO's intangible heritage list in 2020, shared across North Africa

AT.name: Austria
AT.fact: Styrian pumpkin seed oil has been produced in Austria since the 18th century and has protected geographic status

AZ.name: Azerbaijan
AZ.fact: Narsharab, Azerbaijan's pomegranate reduction, is a cornerstone of Azerbaijani cuisine used on fish and meat

BH.name: Bahrain
BH.fact: Baharat, meaning 'spices' in Arabic, is a warm blend central to Gulf cuisine

BT.name: Bhutan
BT.fact: Ema Datshi, Bhutan's national dish, is a fiery stew of chili peppers and yak cheese

BG.name: Bulgaria
BG.fact: Sirene cheese is Bulgaria's signature white brined cheese, protected under EU geographic indication

KH.name: Cambodia
KH.fact: Kroeung is Cambodia's essential aromatic paste, and prahok fermented fish is called 'Cambodian cheese'

CM.name: Cameroon
CM.fact: Ndolé, a stew of bitter leaves and nuts, is Cameroon's national dish

HR.name: Croatia
HR.fact: Istrian truffles rival Italian ones in quality, and Dalmatian prosciutto has centuries of tradition

DK.name: Denmark
DK.fact: Danish smoked herring from Bornholm island has been a delicacy since the Middle Ages

EC.name: Ecuador
EC.fact: Encebollado, a tuna and onion soup, is Ecuador's beloved hangover cure and national comfort food

EG.name: Egypt
EG.fact: Dukka is an Egyptian nut-and-spice blend traditionally eaten by dipping bread in olive oil then into the mix

EE.name: Estonia
EE.fact: Kiluvõileib, Estonia's open-faced sprat sandwich on black bread, is a national staple

ET.name: Ethiopia
ET.fact: Ethiopia is the birthplace of Coffea arabica — legend says a goat herder discovered it around 850 AD

IS.name: Iceland
IS.fact: Icelandic skyr has been made since the Viking age, over 1,000 years ago

IL.name: Israel
IL.fact: Za'atar is a Levantine herb blend so culturally significant it appears in ancient texts

JM.name: Jamaica
JM.fact: Jamaica is the native home of allspice (pimento), the only spice grown exclusively in the Western Hemisphere

JO.name: Jordan
JO.fact: Mansaf, lamb in fermented yogurt sauce, is Jordan's national dish served at celebrations

KE.name: Kenya
KE.fact: Nyama Choma (roasted meat) is Kenya's most popular social food, central to gatherings

LA.name: Laos
LA.fact: Padaek, Lao fermented fish paste, is the backbone of Laotian cuisine — every household makes its own

LV.name: Latvia
LV.fact: Latvian smoked sprat, cured over alder wood, is a Baltic delicacy with centuries of tradition

LB.name: Lebanon
LB.fact: Lebanese cuisine gave the world za'atar, tahini, and pomegranate molasses

LT.name: Lithuania
LT.fact: Cepelinai, Lithuania's national dish, are zeppelin-shaped potato dumplings stuffed with meat

MG.name: Madagascar
MG.fact: Romazava, a meat and leafy greens stew, is considered Madagascar's national dish

MY.name: Malaysia
MY.fact: Malaysian belacan (shrimp paste) and rendang are UNESCO-recognized culinary traditions

ML.name: Mali
ML.fact: Mali is within the Sahel zone where pearl millet was first domesticated thousands of years ago

MA.name: Morocco
MA.fact: Moroccan preserved lemons require 30 days of salt-curing and are essential to tagines

MZ.name: Mozambique
MZ.fact: Mozambique's piri piri sauce blends African bird's eye chilies with Portuguese citrus traditions

NP.name: Nepal
NP.fact: Timur pepper (Sichuan pepper's Himalayan cousin) is native to Nepal and gives a unique numbing tingle

OM.name: Oman
OM.fact: Oman's Dhofar region is one of the few places on Earth where frankincense trees grow wild

PG.name: Papua New Guinea
PG.fact: Papua New Guinea is where sugarcane, plantain, and taro were first domesticated

PT.name: Portugal
PT.fact: Portugal's salt cod (bacalhau) tradition began in the 15th century — they say there are 365 recipes, one for each day

QA.name: Qatar
QA.fact: Qatari cuisine features Baharat and machboos, a spiced rice dish

SA.name: Saudi Arabia
SA.fact: Saudi Kabsa, a spiced rice and meat dish, is the kingdom's most iconic meal

SN.name: Senegal
SN.fact: Yassa, Senegal's tangy lemon-onion sauce, is one of West Africa's most beloved dishes

RS.name: Serbia
RS.fact: Serbian ajvar, a roasted red pepper relish, is made in huge batches every autumn as a family tradition

LK.name: Sri Lanka
LK.fact: Sri Lanka is the only source of true cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum), cultivated for over 2,000 years

SD.name: Sudan
SD.fact: Sudanese ful medames (stewed fava beans) is eaten daily and is one of the oldest known prepared foods

SY.name: Syria
SY.fact: Aleppo pepper, a mild fruity chili, has been cultivated in Syria for centuries

TZ.name: Tanzania
TZ.fact: Pilau Masala, Tanzania's signature spice blend, reflects centuries of Swahili coast trade

TN.name: Tunisia
TN.fact: Tunisian harissa was inscribed on UNESCO's intangible heritage list in 2022

UY.name: Uruguay
UY.fact: Uruguay consumes more mate tea per capita than any other country

VE.name: Venezuela
VE.fact: Venezuelan arepas predate European contact and differ from Colombian versions in preparation

VN.name: Vietnam
VN.fact: Vietnamese fish sauce (nước mắm) is fermented for 12-18 months in wooden barrels

YE.name: Yemen
YE.fact: Yemeni zhug and hawaij spice blends have influenced cuisine across the Horn of Africa

ZW.name: Zimbabwe
ZW.fact: Baobab fruit, native to Zimbabwe's savanna, has six times more vitamin C than oranges
